field_classes.summary: Product-scoped evolution label used within one product lineage.
Machine-readable contract-shape version governed by migration or refusal law.
Machine-readable pin for semantic meaning compatibility.
Deterministic build instance identity such as build_id.
Stable artifact-level identifier distinct from version labels.
Immutable payload identity for exact content.
Later explicit machine-readable release compatibility envelope.
Curated release-control-plane identity for one release snapshot.
Broad compatibility grouping for families of targets.
Machine-readable exact target statement for concrete binary meaning.

field_semantics_summaries.suite_version: Useful for release notes, archive references, and suite snapshots, but not compatibility admission.
field_semantics_summaries.product_version: Useful for product-line progression and deterministic candidate ordering after explicit compatibility gates pass.
field_semantics_summaries.protocol_version_or_range: Governs negotiated wire compatibility and must remain explicit.
field_semantics_summaries.schema_version: Governs structure and migration or refusal obligations.
field_semantics_summaries.format_version: Governs save, manifest, bundle, feed, or archive layout compatibility.
field_semantics_summaries.semantic_contract_bundle_hash: Pins semantic meaning compatibility and CAP-NEG-relevant meaning.
field_semantics_summaries.build_identity: Tracks deterministic build provenance and exact build instance identity without becoming semantic precedence.
field_semantics_summaries.artifact_identity: Tracks stable artifact identity distinct from build and version labels.
field_semantics_summaries.content_hash: Tracks exact payload identity and verification state.
field_semantics_summaries.release_contract_profile: Will later bind the relevant compatibility truths into one explicit release envelope.
field_semantics_summaries.release_id: Names one release snapshot without replacing product, protocol, schema, or build identity fields.
field_semantics_summaries.release_series: Groups release lineage without becoming compatibility truth.
field_semantics_summaries.channel_id: Classifies operator-facing release channels without becoming artifact identity.
field_semantics_summaries.target_family: Supports broad matrix grouping but is insufficient for exact binary compatibility.
field_semantics_summaries.exact_target_descriptor: Carries the specificity required for precise target admission and binary meaning.

notes: Compatibility must be evaluated before product-version ranking or build-identity tie-breaks.
Build identity may participate in deterministic tie-breaks only after compatibility gates pass.
Suite version remains useful for curation but is not the compatibility oracle.

identity_vs_version_distinctions: Version labels describe evolution or compatibility semantics for a defined layer.
Identity fields describe immutable build, artifact, content, or release instance identity.
Build identity and content hash do not replace semantic contract pinning, protocol ranges, schema versions, or format versions.
Artifact identity and release identity are not interchangeable with product version.
target_family_vs_exact_target_distinctio: Target family is a broad grouping for matrix navigation and policy grouping.
Exact target descriptor carries the specificity needed for concrete binary meaning.
Family labels alone are insufficient for exact admission, resolution, or binary compatibility claims.

canonical_sources: authored release engines and resolver code
canonical_vs_derived_surface_rules.rule: Derived or projection surfaces may expose metadata but may not redefine field semantics.

ambiguities_eliminated: suite version is useful but is not the compatibility oracle
build identity does not control semantic precedence
filenames are projections and do not define truth
target family does not equal exact target identity
